content.text: MCP defines three primitives that a server can expose to a host:
(reusable message templates). Knowing which to use for a given capability leads to cleaner server designs and more predictable agent behavior.

attrs.alt: MCP primitives: Tools (callable, side-effects), Resources (read-only, URI), Prompts (templates, messages)

content.text: Choose the primitive that matches the intent: Tools when the agent must act or retrieve live data; Resources when the agent only needs to read static context; Prompts when you want to standardize how the agent approaches a recurring task.
Tools are the most common MCP primitive. They are callable functions that can produce side-effects (write to a database, send a message, create a GitHub issue). The server advertises them via
A tool schema consists of three fields:
: unique identifier used by the client to call the tool.
: natural-language explanation Claude uses to decide when to call the tool.
: JSON Schema object describing the accepted arguments.

Resources expose read-only data as URI-addressed items. Unlike tools, resources have no input schema beyond the URI — the URI itself fully identifies what to read. Resources are appropriate for files, database snapshots, API configuration, and other context the agent should read but not modify.

from mcp.server import Server
from mcp import types
import asyncio, os, pathlib

app = Server("docs-server")
DOCS_ROOT = pathlib.Path(os.environ.get("DOCS_ROOT", "./docs")).resolve()

@app.list_resources()
async def list_resources() -> list[types.Resource]:
    return [
        types.Resource(
            uri=f"docs:///{path.relative_to(DOCS_ROOT)}",
            name=path.stem.replace("-", " ").title(),
            description=f"Documentation: {path.name}",
            mimeType="text/markdown",
        )
        for path in sorted(DOCS_ROOT.rglob("*.md"))
    ]

@app.read_resource()
async def read_resource(uri: str) -> str:
    # Strip scheme, resolve safely within DOCS_ROOT
    rel = uri.removeprefix("docs:///")
    target = (DOCS_ROOT / rel).resolve()
    if not str(target).startswith(str(DOCS_ROOT)):
        raise ValueError(f"Access denied: {uri}")
    return target.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
Reading a resource from the client side:

from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters
from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client
import asyncio

async def read_doc(filename: str) -> str:
    params = StdioServerParameters(command="python", args=["docs_server.py"])
    async with stdio_client(params) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            await session.initialize()

            # List what's available
            catalog = await session.list_resources()
            available = [r.uri for r in catalog.resources]
            target_uri = f"docs:///{filename}"
            if target_uri not in available:
                raise FileNotFoundError(f"{filename} not in docs server")

            # Read the specific document
            result = await session.read_resource(target_uri)
            return "\n".join(c.text for c in result.contents if hasattr(c, "text"))
Resource URIs use a custom scheme (
) that the server defines. The scheme conveys what type of data the URI addresses and lets the server route reads to the right backend.
Prompts are parameterized message templates that a server renders on demand. When the host calls
, the server fetches live context (e.g. a PR from GitHub), injects it into a template, and returns a fully-formed
array. The host passes that array directly to the model.

This differs from tools and resources: Prompts return message arrays, not content blocks. Use them when you want to standardize how the agent frames a recurring task, injecting real data from the server into a consistent instruction format:

from mcp.server import Server
from mcp import types
import asyncio, os, requests

app = Server("review-prompts-server")
_TOKEN = os.environ["GITHUB_TOKEN"]

@app.list_prompts()
async def list_prompts() -> list[types.Prompt]:
    return [
        types.Prompt(
            name="summarize_pr",
            description="Summarize a GitHub pull request for a release note.",
            arguments=[
                types.PromptArgument(name="owner", description="Repo owner", required=True),
                types.PromptArgument(name="repo",  description="Repo name",  required=True),
                types.PromptArgument(name="pr_number", description="PR number", required=True),
            ],
        ),
        types.Prompt(
            name="review_diff",
            description="Review a code diff for bugs and style issues.",
            arguments=[
                types.PromptArgument(name="language", description="Programming language", required=True),
                types.PromptArgument(name="diff",     description="The unified diff text", required=True),
            ],
        ),
    ]

@app.get_prompt()
async def get_prompt(name: str, arguments: dict) -> types.GetPromptResult:
    if name == "summarize_pr":
        owner = arguments["owner"]
        repo  = arguments["repo"]
        pr_num = arguments["pr_number"]

        resp = requests.get(
            f"https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_num}",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {_TOKEN}"},
            timeout=10,
        )
        resp.raise_for_status()
        pr = resp.json()

        return types.GetPromptResult(
            description="PR summary prompt",
            messages=[
                types.PromptMessage(
                    role="user",
                    content=types.TextContent(
                        type="text",
                        text=(
                            f"Write a one-paragraph release note for this pull request:\n\n"
                            f"Title: {pr['title']}\n"
                            f"Author: {pr['user']['login']}\n"
                            f"Description:\n{pr.get('body', 'No description')}\n\n"
                            f"Focus on what changed and why it matters to users."
                        )
                    )
                )
            ]
        )

    if name == "review_diff":
        return types.GetPromptResult(
            description="Code review prompt",
            messages=[
                types.PromptMessage(
                    role="user",
                    content=types.TextContent(
                        type="text",
                        text=(
                            f"Review this {arguments['language']} diff for bugs, security issues, "
                            f"and style violations. Be specific about line numbers.\n\n"
                            f"```{arguments['language']}\n{arguments['diff']}\n```"
                        )
                    )
                )
            ]
        )

    raise ValueError(f"Unknown prompt: {name}")
Using a prompt from the client:

import anthropic
from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters
from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client
import asyncio

claude = anthropic.Anthropic()

async def run_prompt(prompt_name: str, args: dict) -> str:
    params = StdioServerParameters(command="python", args=["review_prompts_server.py"])
    async with stdio_client(params) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            await session.initialize()

            # Fetch the rendered prompt (server fills in dynamic context)
            prompt_result = await session.get_prompt(prompt_name, args)

            # Convert MCP messages to Anthropic API format
            messages = [
                {"role": msg.role, "content": msg.content.text}
                for msg in prompt_result.messages
            ]

            # Pass the ready-made messages directly to Claude
            resp = claude.messages.create(
                model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
                max_tokens=1024,
                messages=messages,
            )
            return resp.content[0].text

# Summarize PR #42 in the anthropics/sdk-python repo
summary = asyncio.run(run_prompt("summarize_pr", {
    "owner": "anthropics", "repo": "sdk-python", "pr_number": "42"
}))
print(summary)
→ Tool (send email, create issue, write file).
→ Resource if the content is stable (config file, doc page); Tool if it requires arguments or is live/dynamic (search results, current metrics).
Is it a recurring instruction pattern?
→ Prompt. Let the server own the template and the context-injection logic; the host just passes the rendered messages to Claude.
Default to Tool. Tools are the most flexible — they can do everything Resources can (read data) plus side-effects, and they're what Claude directly emits
Each primitive has a distinct testing pattern. Tools mock HTTP calls; Resources test path safety and content parsing; Prompts assert that the rendered message array is correct:

import asyncio, pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock
from mcp import types

# --- Testing Tools ---
async def test_list_issues_tool():
    from unittest.mock import patch
    import github_tools_server as srv

    mock_resp = MagicMock()
    mock_resp.json.return_value = [
        {"number": 1, "title": "Bug: crash on startup", "labels": [{"name": "bug"}]},
    ]
    mock_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()

    with patch('github_tools_server.requests.get', return_value=mock_resp):
        result = await srv.call_tool("list_issues", {"owner": "org", "repo": "api"})

    assert "Bug: crash" in result[0].text

# --- Testing Resources ---
async def test_read_resource_safety():
    import docs_server as ds
    import pytest

    # Path traversal should be blocked
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='Access denied'):
        await ds.read_resource('docs:///../../../etc/passwd')

# --- Testing Prompts ---
async def test_get_prompt_renders_messages():
    import review_prompts_server as ps
    from unittest.mock import patch

    mock_pr = {
        "title": "Add streaming support",
        "user": {"login": "alice"},
        "body": "Adds SSE streaming to the API.",
    }
    mock_resp = MagicMock()
    mock_resp.json.return_value = mock_pr
    mock_resp.raise_for_status = MagicMock()

    with patch('review_prompts_server.requests.get', return_value=mock_resp):
        result = await ps.get_prompt('summarize_pr',
                                     {'owner': 'org', 'repo': 'api', 'pr_number': '42'})

    assert len(result.messages) == 1
    assert result.messages[0].role == 'user'
    assert 'streaming' in result.messages[0].content.text
: test path-traversal guards — invalid URIs must raise, not silently succeed.
and that dynamic context (PR title, diff) appears in the rendered text.
: server-rendered message templates. Arguments filled server-side; returns
methods; each follows the same async context-manager client pattern.
Use Tools for actions and live data, Resources for stable readable context, Prompts for standardizing recurring instruction patterns.
